title: Dermatology consultations | Piel en Armonia
description: Dermatology pages with clear explanations, careful decisions, and follow-up that stays grounded.
voiceNote: Each page covers the essentials so you understand what the consultation is for and what usually comes next.

thesis.body: Each consultation summarizes what we observe, what options we usually consider, and what follow-up tends to make sense.

statement.titleTemplate: {service} is discussed first and decided carefully
statement.alt: Clinical direction at Piel en Armonia

ui.internalMessageFallback: The final decision is confirmed in consultation, with examination and clear medical explanation.
ui.faqAnswerNote: General guidance; the final plan is defined during consultation.

bookingStatus.title: Online booking under maintenance
bookingStatus.description: Web booking returns soon. Until then, you can begin through telemedicine.

services.title: Comprehensive diagnosis consultation
services.summary: A broad first consultation to sort findings, priorities, and next steps.
services.lead: The first consultation brings order, clears confusion, and shows what deserves attention before anything else.
faq: What is helpful to bring to the consultation?
Is a diagnosis usually defined during that visit?
When should I return before the planned review?
faqAnswers: It helps to bring medications, previous treatments, allergy history, and recent photos so the consultation starts with useful context.
Often yes; if the case needs more detail, you still leave with a clear working diagnosis and next step.
Move the consultation forward if warning signs appear, pain changes, bleeding starts, or the lesion shifts quickly.

services.title: Acne and rosacea
services.summary: Medical care for flares, sensitivity, and marks with realistic goals.
services.lead: We aim for calmer skin, less inflammation, and a routine that you can realistically keep up.
faq: When do changes usually start to show?
Can it be combined with laser?
What should I do during a strong flare?
faqAnswers: Changes often begin between weeks four and eight when the treatment plan is followed consistently.
Yes, but only once the skin is calmer and the procedure timing will not push irritation higher.
If the flare worsens, write in or bring the review forward so the plan changes before more marks appear.

services.title: Warts
services.summary: Wart care based on location, thickness, and tendency to come back.
services.lead: The technique is chosen by wart type and location so treatment does not punish healthy skin.
faq: How many sessions are usually needed?
Is treatment painful?
Can the wart come back?
faqAnswers: Some improve in one or two sessions, while deeper plantar warts often need more follow-up and patience.
There can be brief discomfort, but the procedure is adjusted so it stays tolerable and safe for that area.
Yes, recurrence can happen, especially when the lesion is deep or the skin keeps facing exposure.

services.title: Bumps on arms and legs
services.summary: Consultation for rough texture, visible follicles, and small recurring bumps.
services.heroAlt: Clinical care for rough skin texture
services.lead: Barrier care and follicular treatment work together to improve texture without overworking the skin.
faq: Is this always keratosis pilaris?
How long does texture improvement take?
Which products are best avoided?
faqAnswers: Not always; we also look for eczema, folliculitis, or other causes that can look similar at first.
Improvement is often felt between six and ten weeks when the routine stays steady and simple.
It is best to avoid harsh exfoliants, very drying soaps, and heavy fragrance if the area is reactive.

services.title: Scars
services.summary: A staged plan to improve contour, color, and texture in scars.
services.lead: Meaningful scar improvement usually comes in layers, with honest timing and combinations matched to scar type.
faq: Do all scars respond in the same way?
How many sessions are often suggested?
Which habits help most afterwards?
faqAnswers: No; scar age, scar type, skin tone, and response over time all shape how the plan performs.
Several sessions are common, but the exact number changes with progress and treatment tolerance.
Daily sun protection, good hydration, and avoiding early friction help preserve what has been gained.

services.title: Skin cancer
services.summary: Early evaluation for lesions that change or raise concern.
services.lead: When a mole or lesion changes, the priority is reviewing it soon and deciding without avoidable delay.
faq: How often should moles be reviewed?
Which signs call for prompt consultation?
Can this start through telemedicine?
faqAnswers: It depends on personal risk, but many people need periodic review when there is history or prior findings.
Seek consultation promptly for asymmetry, irregular borders, bleeding, lasting itch, or a fast color change.
Telemedicine can help set priority, although many lesions still need direct examination during consultation.

services.title: Chemical peel
services.summary: Supervised renewal for spots, texture, and some surface marks.
services.lead: The peel is adjusted to your skin tone, sensitivity, and the result we are realistically trying to reach.
faq: How many days does recovery usually take?
Can it be combined with laser?
Which situations make us wait?
faqAnswers: Recovery usually lasts three to seven days, depending on peel depth and how your skin responds.
It can, but only when session timing protects the skin and keeps irritation from stacking up.
We wait when there is active irritation, pregnancy, or little chance of keeping sun protection well.

services.title: Mesotherapy
services.summary: Short sessions that improve hydration, glow, and skin quality.
services.lead: It helps when we want localized support and better skin quality without long or heavy procedures.
faq: Is there any downtime?
How often is it usually repeated?
Which treatments pair well with it?
faqAnswers: There may be mild redness for a day or two, but most people return to routine very quickly.
A series of sessions spaced by weeks is common, depending on the response that begins to show.
It often pairs well with medical hydration, gentle peels, or staged rejuvenation plans.

services.title: Dermatology laser
services.summary: Laser treatments with medical settings reviewed at every session.
services.lead: Every setting is defined by clinical goal and by real skin response, never by a fixed recipe.
faq: Does it work for every skin tone?
How many sessions are usually needed?
Which aftercare cannot be skipped?
faqAnswers: Yes, but settings shift greatly between skin tones, which is why careful evaluation matters first.
The number depends on the problem being treated; pigment, vessels, and scars each move at different speeds.
After laser we insist on sun protection, gentle care, and closer review if irritation appears.

services.title: Medical botox
services.summary: Medical treatment for expression lines that keeps a natural look.
services.lead: The aim is to refresh expression without erasing movement or creating a look that feels unlike you.
faq: When does the change begin to show?
How often is it usually repeated?
Which same-day care matters most?
faqAnswers: Early change is often noticed within days, with fuller effect appearing around the second week.
For many people it lasts around three or four months, though timing changes by area and metabolism.
During the first day it is best to avoid heavy massage, intense heat, or demanding exercise.

services.title: Collagen biostimulators
services.summary: Gradual support for firmness when the skin begins to lose structure.
services.lead: We plan by area to improve support and skin quality through gradual changes that still feel natural.
faq: How long before changes can be noticed?
How many sessions are often suggested?
Can it be paired with medical botox?
faqAnswers: Change is not immediate; it usually starts to show after several weeks and keeps building over time.
Two or three sessions are common, although the exact number depends on laxity and individual response.
Yes, it can be part of a broader facial plan when timing is organized carefully.

services.title: Skin, hair and nails
services.summary: A broad consultation for mixed symptoms involving skin, hair, and nails.
services.lead: When several symptoms appear together, it helps to read the full picture instead of treating separate pieces.
faq: Which situations usually fit here?
Can it begin through telemedicine?
How often is it reviewed afterward?
faqAnswers: It fits hair shedding, nail changes, and skin symptoms that overlap or last for a long time.
Yes; telemedicine can help organize the start, although some cases benefit quickly from direct examination.
Follow-up is often planned between four and eight weeks, depending on findings and early response.

services.title: Pediatric dermatology
services.summary: Care for children and adolescents with clear explanations for the family.
services.lead: We translate medical decisions into steps that are manageable at home, at school, and in daily life.
faq: What is helpful to bring to the first visit?
How is the plan explained to the family?
Can it be combined with telemedicine?
faqAnswers: It helps to bring a symptom summary, creams already used, allergy history, and recent photos.
We explain it in plain language and turn it into clear tasks for home and school.
Yes, it can help between visits when there is no fever, intense pain, or other warning signs.
